feed.title: AO3 - Clark Kent/Bruce Wayne (Filtered)
feed.subtitle: Latest works for Clark Kent/Bruce Wayne on Archive of Our Own [English, No ABO]

entry.title: What Would You Trade the Pain For?
entry.summary:
Author: theFamousLivingDead

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 4,759Chapters: 2/?Kudos: 16

Summary: D: Got a job for you. On one condition?B: What condition?D: Clark tags along.B: No.D: Bruce. It's the only condition.B: Send Hal. Barry. Anyone else. After a disastrous Justice League mission, Clark Kent and Bruce Wayne are at each other's throats. Literally. They both mean well, but cannot appreciate each other's bothersome influences. When Lex Luthor escapes to a town where the Justice League can't follow, Diana calls upon Bruce to go undercover. But he's not allowed to go on his own. Or.. Bruce and Clark get a chance to mend whatever relationship they have by playing pretend and who knows, they might end up being great actors after all!

  • No Archive Warnings Apply

entry.title: I'm on my knees for you
entry.summary:
Author: guineapiguniversestuff

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 2,840Chapters: 1/1Kudos: 33

Summary: "Clark knew him inside and out; he knew what his heartbeat sounded like at rest and what it sounded like when he was close to unraveling. Clark knew how to play Bruce, and he had every chance to completely ruin the man beneath him, but instead all he wanted to do was please him. He wanted to serve and satisfy Bruce so much that it hurt him.”

entry.title: After Many Springs
entry.summary:
Author: Burning_Sands999

Rating: MatureLanguage: EnglishWords: 12,162Chapters: 6/?Kudos: 93

Summary: This man looks like Clark. He sounds like Clark. The look on his face is identical to the one Clark wore when his favorite cow had been put down on the farm several years ago. But he can’t be Clark. Clark is gone. Clark has been gone. Clark isn’t coming back. Or:On a seemingly normal day in 2027, Superman disappears.Three years later, he comes back with no memory of ever leaving.This is the fallout.

entry.title: Second time is the charm
entry.summary:
Author: Emelian65

Rating: Teen And Up AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 27,739Chapters: 11/?Kudos: 258

Summary: Clark wakes up in his bed, feeling strange. He had vivid dreams of a cataclysmic future. He sits up, tries to shake it off, but the memories are too detailed, overwhelming. He remembers the world ending, heroes falling, his own death? But then he's alive, young. He checks the date... It's now 2011, several years in the past.

entry.title: Despite everything, it’s still you
entry.summary:
Author: VettTrece

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 88,521Chapters: 15/18Kudos: 490

Summary: Bruce Wayne, lonely, distrustful, and worn down by a city that never changes, witnesses the impossible: a man flies in front of him, no wires, no tricks, no logical explanation. Superman has arrived. Not as the man from Bruce's world, but as a figure who shouldn’t have crossed the threshold. He hails from a different universe, one with different rules. A place where, according to his logic, Bruce is meant to be his soulmate. But in this Gotham, there is no destiny, there is no hope. In his home there's no room for myths. Until now. Or: Clark isn't in his own world: the one where soulmates are as natural as the warmth of the sun or the darkness of night. He doesn't know how he ended up here; perhaps fate, perhaps chance, but undeniably the Gotham's vigilante spoke the first words destiny had reserved for him. And yet, everything feels strange, like a distorted reflection of the reality he once knew. Bruce, unaware of the burden Clark carries, chooses to help him find a way back to his world, and along the way, to uncover more about his own reality.Léelo en Español aquí.

entry.title: All The Things We Cannot See
entry.summary:
Author: Ivasa

Rating: Teen And Up AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 17,157Chapters: 5/?Kudos: 57

Summary: People disappear in Gotham every day. Batman finds them. That’s the job. But information has a price, and sometimes it requires becoming someone else entirely. A different face, a different name, a different life - Bruce is good at it. To him, wearing a disguise is almost easier than being himself. This case should have been simple. It stops being simple the moment a rookie reporter from Metropolis decides that Matches Malone is exactly the kind of source he’s been looking for.

entry.summary:
Author: i8thecookie

Rating: Teen And Up AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 14,230Chapters: 6/?Kudos: 112

Summary: Years ago, in Smallville, Bruce made an impression on Clark. And not a good one. Now Bruce is back in his life—charming, relentless, and completely unaware of the damage he left behind all those years ago. But Clark hasn't forgotten. And while Batman and Superman learn how to trust each other, their civilian identities are busy getting everything wrong.

entry.title: The Weight of Never Leaving
entry.summary:
Author: Holdt

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 6,330Chapters: 2/?Kudos: 6

Summary: Jason Todd has been dead for six hours and is already at the garden wall. Martha Wayne runs the perimeter. Thomas holds the suppression. The new ghost doesn't wait for orientation — he has three years of field intelligence and a shit list and unfinished business with every name on it. Above them, Bruce Wayne in the cave listening to Morrow's anniversary digging for the first time with his eyes open. Clark Kent in the city, within reach, for now. Alfred Pennyworth with medication on the nightstand and a very specific idea of where he wants to die. The Weight of Never Leaving is the third book of The House We Never Leave. It is the one where the fracture completes, the talon surfaces, a fourteen-year-old with a surveillance file and no survival instincts rings the doorbell at dusk, and Alfred Pennyworth dies in his chosen place. Clark was right. Bruce chooses the house anyway. He stands at the window. He does not run after him. Every heartbeat hurts.

  • Creator Chose Not To Use Archive Warnings

entry.title: Perihelion
entry.summary:
Author: Holdt

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 15,582Chapters: 9/10Kudos: 29

Summary: Two years after the gala. The orbit has found its shape. Dinners almost every night. Out three times a week. Saturdays are films. Sundays are newspapers and coffee and not checking the time. The mug is on the left side of the kitchen. The shoes are in the hallway. The arm is still there every morning. Every day is a good day. Bruce has questions he's been sitting on since the east wall. Clark has answers — some of them. The ones that are his to give. There's also the small matter of what Bruce says out loud at the Kerth ceremony. In front of witnesses. — Book Two of the Aphelion trilogy. Established relationship. Explicit throughout. The bruises are a love language now. Both directions.
